title: Achala Fort
tagline: Achala Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Achala Fort is a westernmost fort in the Satmala hill range.
overview.description: Achala Fort is a westernmost fort in the Satmala hill range. It is located 55 km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra. This fort is adjoining the Ahivant Fort. The three forts Achala, Ahivant, and Mohandar are very close by.

topPlaces.name: Ahivant Fort

topPlaces.name: Punegaon Dam
topPlaces.description: Punegaon Dam, is an earthfill dam on Unanda river near Punegaon, Vani, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Nanduri Dam
topPlaces.description: Nanduri Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Tapti river near Ama Local Nallaher, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

Punegaon Dam, is an earthfill dam on Unanda river near Punegaon, Vani, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Achala Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Achala Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Achala Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (54 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Achala Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Achala Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Achala Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Achala Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Achala Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ahivant Fort, Punegaon Dam, Nanduri Dam.
